purpose: Identify which FX factors survive rigorous multiple-testing thresholds (Harvey-Liu-Zhu) to distinguish genuine risk premia from data-mined noise.
components: Dollar factor (PC1): survives with robust out-of-sample evidence
Carry factor: t-stat 3.5-5.0 across specifications — survives
PPP value factor: t-stat 1.5-2.5 — marginal, fails threshold
Spanning regressions: exotic factors subsumed by carry + momentum
current_assessment: Three-factor model (dollar, carry, momentum) is the empirically defensible minimum. Multi-factor FX products based on exotic factors (output gap, external balance, Taylor rule residual) are selling noise. Static diversified factor harvesting with vol targeting dominates dynamic timing for most institutional horizons.
limitations: HLZ is conservative and may over-prune factors with genuine but weaker signals
Three surviving factors collapse to ~1.2-1.5 effective factors during stress (PCA collapse)
Factor timing skepticism: theoretical IR 0.3-0.5 degrades to implementable 0.1-0.2
